rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN RE NOB HILL LAMBOURNE HOMEOWNERS'' ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON THE PETITION TO AUTHORIZE APPROVAL OF AMENDMENTS TO DECLARATION OF COVENANTS, CONDITIONS AND RESTRICTIONS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2025 line 1. HEARING ON THE PETITION TO AUTHORIZE APPROVAL OF AMENDMENTS TO DECLARATION OF COVENANTS, CONDITIONS AND RESTRICTIONS is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES MARVEL ET AL VS. GABRIEL J. HOLMES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF FRANCES MARVEL , AN INDIVIDUAL, SIMON VILHELMSSON DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, and MOOT in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for equitable indemnity is moot. Dismissal on file. Demurrer to the cause of action for contribution is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend to identify a joint tort asserted/or that could be asserted by the owner against the master tenant and subtenant for which both of them may potentially become judgement debtors at the end of this litigation arising out of the fact alleged in the Owner's Cross-Complaint. Despite the Court's prior ruling on May 7, 2024 to identify a joint tort, Bungalow Living failed to do so. Paragraphs 15-17 identify none despite the opposition's assertions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 690 MARKET CLUB OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. 690 MARKET MASTER ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Of Defendants Michael Mayer And Stephen Perlman
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT STEPHEN PERLMAN , AN INDIVIDUAL, MICHAEL MAYER Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Of Defendants Michael Mayer And Stephen Perlman is GRANTED, in part. No opposition filed. Defendants are awarded $51,794.25 in attorney's fees in connection with the anti-slapp motion. The requested fee award was reduced by $59.50 because two attorneys billed to review the same tentative ruling. Mr. Brown's fees were also excluded. Counsel failed to provide authority that fees incurred by a legal assistant are recoverable. With respect to the fees incurred in connection with the attorney fee motion, the court finds that 12.5 hours is unreasonable. The court will credit counsel with half, 5 hours for Ms. Campbell's time and 1 hour for Mr. Riggs time, for a total award of $55,664.25 for attorney's fees. With respect to costs. Defendants are awarded $1,036.90. The Court cannot discern what filing fees Defendants incurred on 11/25/24 for $19.36 and on 1/14/25 for $21.42. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAM SHI LEE VS. EUGENE JUN KWOCK TOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement And Request For Stay Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2025 line 8. DEFENDANT EUGENE TOM Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgement And Request For Stay Of Execution is DENIED. Defendant has not shown that his failure to attend the trial of this action was due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Request for stay of execution is moot as the judgment has already been executed and as such there is no pending execution for the Court to stay. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
